LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN O'BRIEN, AND, CONSTANCE O'BRIEN MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S PRAYER FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO GIVE NOTICE, PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 8.3. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DRIVING WITH A SUSPENDED LICENSE DOES NOT RISE TO THE LEVEL OF OPPRESSION, FRAUD, OR MALICE TO SUPPORT PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike On Behalf Of Constance O'Brien And John O'Brien;
Off Calendar Sep-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOHN O'BRIEN, and, CONSTANCE O'BRIEN Motion to Strike Plaintiff's prayer for exemplary damages. Appearances: Timothy P. Leibel, Plaintiff in Pro Per and opposing party appearing. Plaintiff failed to give notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend. Driving with a suspended license does not rise to the level of oppression, fraud, or malice to support punitive damages. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOHN O'BRIEN, and, CONSTANCE O'BRIEN DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Timothy P. Leibel, Plaintiff in Pro Per and opposing party appearing. Plaintiff failed to give notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend for plaintiff to dismiss the insurer(s) and delete references to them. Plaintiff may not sue both the insured and the insurer in the same action. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
